mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.22% represents a restrictive monetary stance relative to inflation (CPI at 323.364). Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS signals the Fed maintains tightening bias, which compresses near-term equity valuations and increases real discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher term premiums over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL growth rate exceeds 2.0% annualized
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-trend inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20-day forward window despite FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPI growth > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.3% signals tight labor market conditions. Sustained low UNRATE below 4.5% indicates wage pressure and inflation persistence, forcing the Fed to maintain or increase policy rates, which drives 10-year Treasury yields upward as market prices in longer-term rate expectations over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 20-day forward window despite UNRATE < 4.5%, or positive forward returns occur in > 65% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5%, contradicting the yield-rise prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 732,010M) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,307K) both remain elevated, signaling robust aggregate demand. Sustained demand growth forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer, increasing the expected path of future short rates and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 732,010M and HOUST at 1,307K as of t-2mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines despite both RSAFS growth > 0.5% and HOUST > 1,200K, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 103.92) shows moderate growth. A deceleration in INDPRO below 103.5 over the next 20 trading days signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential economic slowdown, causing investors to reduce inflation expectations and demand lower yields, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward.
regime_hint: early-stage economic slowdown or supply-side weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite INDPRO decline > 0.3%, or negative forward returns occur in < 45% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 159,540K) represents cumulative labor market strength. A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS signals labor market deterioration, which reduces inflation expectations and prompts Fed rate-cut expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run policy easing.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or post-shock recovery
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 5-day forward window despite PAYEMS decline > 50K, or positive forward returns occur in > 60% of instances when PAYEMS declines > 50K.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 23,770.976B) growth trajectory combined with elevated HOUST signals sustained demand. If GDPC1 growth accelerates above 2.5% annualized while HOUST remains above 1,250K, the Fed signals extended tightening, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in a prolonged restrictive cycle.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate > 2.5% AND HOUST > 1,250K
regime_hint: sustained late-cycle expansion with strong demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 60-day window despite GDPC1 growth > 2.5% and HOUST > 1,250K, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL at 323.364) combined with elevated FEDFUNDS creates a real rate environment. If CPI growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, real rates become restrictively high, signaling potential policy error. Markets anticipate Fed rate cuts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized growth rate < 2.0% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 323.364 and FEDFUNDS at 4.22% as of t-2mo and t-1mo
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 60-day window despite CPI growth < 2.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 103.92) serves as a real-time supply indicator. Sustained INDPRO growth above 104.0 combined with elevated PAYEMS signals robust supply-side capacity and labor availability, reducing inflation expectations and allowing the Fed to pause tightening, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.92 and PAYEMS at 159,540K as of t-2mo
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 20-day window despite INDPRO > 104.0 and PAYEMS growth > 150K, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
